Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event managed by Mashup Music Hub.

dayOfWeek.description: Day of the week the event takes place.

venue.description: Location or venue of the event.

client.description: Name of the client or contractor for the event.

totalValue.description: Total value of the event contract.

downPayment.description: Amount of the down payment received.

paymentStatus.description: Current payment status of the event (e.g., pending, paid, overdue).

accountId.description: Reference to Account. (Relationship: Account 1:N Event). The account that received the value for the event.

djIds.description: References to DJs. (Relationship: DJ N:N Event). List of DJs assigned to the event.

DJ.title: DJ
DJ.description: Represents a DJ in the Mashup Music Hub agency.

percentage.description: Percentage of event revenue the DJ receives.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a bank or payment account used for event transactions.

name.description: Name of the account (e.g., Bank of America Checking).

Settlement.title: Settlement
Settlement.description: Represents a financial settlement for a DJ, generated for a specific period.

djId.description: Reference to DJ. (Relationship: DJ 1:N Settlement). The DJ this settlement is for.

amount.description: Total amount to be paid to the DJ.

notes.description: Any notes or observations related to the settlement.

receiptUrl.description: URL of the uploaded payment receipt.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the Mashup Music Hub platform. Note: Does NOT store authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

role.description: Role of the user (e.g., admin, partner, dj, finance, manager/producer).

djId.description: Reference to DJ. (Relationship: DJ 1:1 UserProfile).  If the user is a DJ, this is the reference to their DJ record.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document associated with an event, stored in Firebase Storage.

eventId.description: Reference to Event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Document). The event this document is associated with.

storageUrl.description: URL of the document in Firebase Storage.

uploadDate.description: Date and time the document was uploaded.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Indicates admin privileges for a user. Existence of a document grants admin access.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores event information. Includes denormalized 'members' map for authorization independence.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the event.

definition.description: Stores DJ information. Used to associate DJs with events and settlements.

params.name: djId
params.description: The unique identifier for the DJ.

definition.description: Stores settlement information for DJs.  Path-based ownership ensures that only the DJ and authorized personnel can access the settlement data.

params.name: djId
params.description: The unique identifier for the DJ.

params.name: settlementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the settlement.

definition.description: Stores documents associated with an event.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique identifier for the event.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document.

definition.description: Stores financial account information.  Access should be restricted to finance and admin roles.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the financial account.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the Mashup Music Hub application, focusing on secure, scalable, and maintainable data management. The design emphasizes authorization independence through denormalization, clear segregation of data based on access requirements, and consistent access modeling.  This design addresses the core features: user authentication with role-based access control, event management, and DJ financial system.

Consistent access modeling is applied throughout the structure. Private data related to users is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, ensuring path-based ownership. Collaborative data, like events, leverages the denormalized 'members' map to define access control.

To support role-based access control, a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{uid}` is used.  The existence of a document with the user's UID in this collection grants admin privileges, and this simplifies rules for admin-level access.

This structure supports all application use cases, including:

*   **User Authentication and Roles**: User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}/profile`, with a `role` field indicating their permissions. Admins can be identified via `/roles_admin/{userId}`.
*   **Event Management**: Events are stored in `/events/{eventId}`.  DJ assignments are managed within the `event` document via the `djIds` property.
*   **DJ Financial System**: Settlement documents are stored under `/djs/{djId}/settlements/{settlementId}`. This structure ensures that only the DJ and authorized personnel can access the settlement data.
